title: centaurea hohenackeri
intro: Centaurea hohenackeri, also known as the Alpine Knapweed, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Centaurea hohenackeri include its ability to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of Centaurea hohenackeri include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the plant's flowers, leaves, and roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
